stories.headline: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns

stories.headline: Trump threatens tariffs on 60 trading partners including UK and Canada over ‘forced labour’

stories.headline: US floats new tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor, including China
US tariff threats over forced labour 'unjustified', Commission says
stories.summary: The EU hit back at US threats of fresh tariffs over forced labour, saying its rules are among the most stringent in the world for banning imports and exports of goods linked to forced labour.

stories.headline: Bolivian ministers resign as weeks of protests against economic crisis rock government
stories.summary: The government has refused to rule out declaring a state of emergency and using the military to control the demonstrations.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
stories.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

stories.headline: EU Allows Room for Energy Crisis Aid, But With a Green Emphasis
stories.summary: The European Union will give extra budget leeway to member states to cope with high energy prices stoked by the Iran war, so long as it helps curb consumption of fossil fuels.

stories.headline: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
stories.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

stories.headline: Eight killed after drone hits bus in Russian-occupied part of Ukraine

stories.headline: Vanguard’s VOO Hits $1 Trillion of Assets in ETF Industry First
stories.summary: A seemingly endless appetite for buying US stock dips has propelled Vanguard Group’s S&P 500-tracking ETF past $1 trillion in assets, making it the first fund of its kind to reach a milestone once thought unimaginable for the ETF industry.

stories.headline: Ares CEO Arougheti Says the Private Credit Market Isn't Broken
stories.summary: Ares Management co-founder and CEO Michael Arougheti says the private credit market remains strong and the recent stress in the private credit market is tied to private equity. He speaks with Dani Burger at the Forbes Iconoclast Summit in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Gains as US-Iran Clashes Cloud Peace Deal Outlook
stories.summary:
Oil rose for a third day as a fresh exchange of strikes between the US and Iran cast doubt on the prospects of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Leslie Palti-Guzman, founder at Energy Vista, gives an update on the outlook for oil and natural gas prices amid the war in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: S&P 500 Tops 7,600 as AI Fuels Nine-Day Win Streak
stories.summary:
A renewed advance in oil sent stocks lower as bond yields rose on concern that an escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran will hinder prospects for a peace deal. 
As equities fell from all-time highs, the S&P 500 snapped a nine-day winning streak. Tech shares led losses, with a closely watched ETF tracking software firms dropping 3.5%.
Dean Curnutt, founder at Macro Risk Advisors, discusses how the markets are reacting to geopolitic

stories.headline: Ares CEO Arougheti on Private Credit, Sports Investing
stories.summary: Ares Management co-founder and CEO Michael Arougheti says the recent stress in the private credit market is tied to private equity. He speaks with Dani Burger at the Forbes Iconoclast Summit in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Credit Titans Warn of Shakeout on Deals That ‘Don’t Make Sense’
stories.summary: Beneath the surface of buoyant credit markets, a shakeout is building for leveraged deals made years ago, as well as for direct-lending funds that are grappling with an extended period of investor withdrawals.

stories.headline: Out of Time to Delay, House to Vote on Trump’s Power to Continue War in Iran
stories.summary: A measure to direct the president to halt U.S. engagement in Iran had been on track to pass in late May, but Republican leaders postponed action. They have run out of time to delay the vote.

stories.headline: 5 Takeaways From the California Primary Election
stories.summary: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.

stories.headline: Nine takeaways from a big primary night in Iowa, California and more
stories.summary: In Iowa, Josh Turek won the Democratic nomination for Senate, defeating a rival who had criticized party leaders. Major races in California were not yet called.

stories.headline: Is Asia facing a new currency crisis?
stories.summary: A stronger dollar, higher energy costs and market uncertainty are putting pressure on currencies across Asia.

Meliá and Iberostar quit Cuba: Trump's ultimatum leaves Spanish hotel groups under pressure
stories.summary: Meliá has announced that it is halting operations at 15 Cuban hotels. Iberostar did so on 1 June at 12 hotels, bowing to US sanctions on Gaesa, the military group controlling tourism.

stories.headline: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show

stories.headline: Inotiv, Breeder of Laboratory Animals, Files For Bankruptcy
stories.summary: Inotiv Inc., which breeds dogs and imports monkeys that are used in drug experiments, filed for bankruptcy in part on what it blamed are tighter rules on its US canine farm and tariffs on non-human primates raised in China.

stories.headline: Treasuries Eye Biggest Loss in Two Weeks After Jobs Gauge
stories.summary: Treasuries headed for their biggest drop in more than two weeks after a gauge of private-sector employment growth was in line with estimates, leaving intact expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates this year.

stories.headline: NatWest Names Roose Interim Head of Primary Capital Markets
stories.summary: NatWest Group Plc has appointed Scott Roose as interim head of its primary capital markets business in a leadership change at the unit.

stories.headline: Private Credit Marks Drawing More Scrutiny From SDNY Prosecutors
stories.summary: Wall Street’s top prosecutor said his office is looking at possible valuation discrepancies in the private credit marketplace.

stories.headline: Private Credit Stocks Fall After Cliffwater Redemption Requests
stories.summary: Shares of the biggest alternative asset managers tumbled Wednesday after Cliffwater LLC’s flagship private credit fund reported even larger redemption requests than in the first quarter, setting off a new wave of fears about the industry.

stories.summary: Crypto finance conglomerate Galaxy Digital Inc. has launched a trading desk to offer large investors better access to prediction markets via over-the-counter derivatives similar to those used in markets from interest rates to commodities.

stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.
